overall_assessment: The final-baseline RR-AUTH-04 packet provides strong real-backend regression coverage, assertion realism, and CI-equivalent validation across the behavior-defining auth, reset, and risk-matrix paths. The documented test evidence is sufficient for this lane, with one remaining material residual concern: the packet still lacks preserved fail-first provenance and continues to rely on explicit substitute-evidence debt.
recommended_path: Accept the final-baseline rerun as sufficient on test effectiveness, realism, and coverage depth, keep the historical fail-first provenance gap recorded as explicit accepted verification debt, and proceed with the remaining closure-only review and orchestration gates without reopening implementation solely for that gap.

findings.title: Historical fail-first provenance remains the only material residual test-quality debt
findings.rationale: Both the bounded package and governing TODO explicitly state that preserved fail-first or red-run artifacts do not exist because RR-AUTH-04 was normalized after code and test hardening had already begun. The current packet substantially reduces closure risk through named regression inventory, focused and impacted-auth reruns, architecture guardrails, and a green full Laravel CI-equivalent suite, but it still cannot prove the current assertions would have failed on the pre-fix implementation.
findings.suggested_action: Carry this forward as explicit accepted verification debt in the closure ledger unless preserved red-run evidence can later be reconstructed, but do not block closure-grade test-quality assessment on that basis alone because the final-baseline packet now provides broad real-backend substitute coverage.
